title: Handset for occlusion clearing device
abstract: A handset for operation and locking of an occlusion clearing device includes a body and movable cover. Valve(s) in fluid communication with tubing for aspiration or irrigation are located within the body. A locking mechanism having first and second components affixed at different points in the handset which are selectively movable relative to one another to transition between locked and unlocked positions. The first component is connected to and selectively movable relative to the cover. The second component engages a portion of the first component, causing the cover to move in an engagement direction as the first component is moved to a locked position. Movement of the cover in an engagement direction causes the valve(s) to change from a first to second position which controls the flow of fluid therethrough. An engagement surface of the second component engages and retains the first component in a locked position.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A handset, comprising: a body; a cover connected and movable relative to said body between a disengaged position and an engaged position; a locking mechanism comprising: (i) a first component mounted to said cover and selectively movable between an unlocked position and a locked position, causing said cover to move between said disengaged position and said engaged position; (ii) a second component affixed to said body and configured to engage at least a portion of said first component, causing said cover to move between said disengaged position and said engaged position as said first component moves between said unlocked and locked positions, and further configured to selectively retain said first component in said locked position; and at least one valve located within said body and operable between a first position and a second position to control flow of fluid through said body, wherein movement of said cover between said disengaged position and said engaged position causes said at least one valve to change between said first position and said second position. 2. The handset as recited in claim 1, wherein said second component is configured to retain said first component in said locked position by frictional force. 3. The handset as recited in claim 2, wherein application of force sufficient to overcome said frictional force is required to selectively move said first component from said locked position to said unlocked position. 4. The handset as recited in claim 1, wherein said second component includes an engagement surface dimensioned to contact and restrict movement said first component in said locked position. 5. The handset as recited in claim 4, wherein said at least one valve includes a biasing member configured to provide biasing force to said first component against said engagement surface in a counter-force direction in said locked position. 6. The handset as recited in claim 1, wherein said second component includes a receiver having an incline angle relative to said cover, said receiver dimensioned to receive and restrain at least a portion of said first component therein and to direct said first component along said incline angle as said first component moves between said unlocked and locked positions. 7. The handset as recited in claim 6, wherein said incline angle is up to 90Â° relative to said cover. 8. The handset as recited in claim 7, wherein said incline angle is in the range of about 35Â° - 75Â°. 9. The handset as recited in claim 6, wherein said first component includes an extension protruding from said first component, said receiver dimensioned to receive and restrain said extension therein. 10. The handset as recited in claim 9, wherein said receiver further comprises an engagement surface dimensioned to contact and restrict movement said extension in said locked position. 11. The handset as recited in claim 1, further comprising tubing in fluid flow communication with said at least one valve and extending through said body, said tubing configured and dimensioned to transport said fluid therethrough. 12. The handset as recited in claim 11, further comprising irrigation tubing extending through said body and dimensioned to transport irrigant therethrough; a first valve in fluid flow communication with said irrigation tubing and operable between said first and second positions to control flow of irrigant therethrough; aspiration tubing extending through said body and dimensioned to transport aspirant therethrough; and a second valve in fluid flow communication with said aspiration tubing and operable between said first and second positions to control flow of aspirant therethrough; wherein movement of said cover between said disengaged and engaged positions causes each of said first and second valves to change between said first and second positions. 13. The handset as recited in claim 12, wherein said first and second valves are configured to change between said first and second positions concurrently. 14. The handset as recited in claim 12, wherein said first position restricts fluid flow through said first and second valves and said second position permits fluid flow through said first and second valves. 15. The handset as recited in claim 12, further comprising a tubing junction having a first passage in fluid flow communication with said aspiration tubing; a second passage in fluid flow communication with said irrigation tubing; and a third passage in fluid flow communication with said first and second passages. 16. The handset as recited in claim 15, wherein said handset further comprises a motor generating reciprocating motion, and a shaft mechanically connecting said motor and said tubing junction. 17. An occlusion clearing device, comprising: a handset as recited in claim 12; a clearing stem having: (i) an operative distal end and an opposite proximal end selectively connectable to said handset; (ii) an aspiration conduit defining an aspiration lumen therein, providing aspiration from said operative distal end, and having at least one opening at said operative distal end; (iii) an irrigation conduit positioned interior to and terminating within said aspiration lumen, said irrigation conduit defining an irrigation lumen therein; wherein said aspiration lumen is connectable in fluid communication with said aspiration tubing of said handset and said irrigation lumen is connectable in fluid communication with said irrigation tubing of said handset. 18. The occlusion clearing device as recited in claim 17, wherein said handset further comprises a motor generating reciprocating motion, and a shaft mechanically connecting said motor and said proximal end of said clearing stem. 19. An occlusion clearing device, comprising: a handset as recited in claim 15; a clearing stem having: 

 (i) an operative distal end and an opposite proximal end selectively connectable to said handset; (ii) an aspiration conduit defining an aspiration lumen therein, configured to provide aspiration from said operative distal end, and having at least one opening at said operative distal end; (iii) an irrigation conduit positioned interior to and terminating within said aspiration lumen, said irrigation conduit defining an irrigation lumen therein; wherein said proximal end of said clearing stem is connectable in fluid communication with said third passage of said tubing junction. 20. The occlusion clearing device as recited in claim 19, wherein said handset further comprises a motor generating reciprocating motion, and a shaft mechanically connecting said motor and said tubing junction.
